chunk_summary: Pediatric cholestasis requires tailored macronutrient targets, including higher energy, protein, and fat needs, with a balanced MCT/LCT approach and avoidance of prolonged fasting to prevent hypoglycemia and malnutrition.
learning_objectives: Understand macronutrient requirements for pediatric cholestasis, including energy, protein, and fat targets.
Learn the importance of balancing MCT and LCT fat intake to prevent EFAD and optimize growth.
Recognize strategies to prevent hypoglycemia in infants with cholestasis, including frequent feeds and overnight feeding.
key_facts: Energy needs are approximately 130% of age requirements, with REE measurement preferred when available.
Fat should provide 30–50% of total calories, with a starting MCT/LCT ratio of 30%/70%.
Avoid MCT fractions >80% of fat energy to prevent EFAD and impaired weight gain.
Protein targets are 130–150% of age requirements; low-protein diets are contraindicated in end-stage liver disease.
Carbohydrates should provide 40–60% of calories, balancing hypoglycemia risk and insulin resistance.
Infants under 6 months require frequent feeds and bedtime snacks to avoid prolonged fasting and hypoglycemia.
reference_quote: Fat commonly provides 30–50% of total calories. Start with a mixed MCT/LCT approach (about 30%/70% of fat calories), increasing MCT when growth is suboptimal or LCT is poorly tolerated.
document_key_topics: Energy and protein requirements in chronic liver disease
Fasting avoidance strategies for hypoglycemia prevention
